notes: Run against a live docker-compose stack the user brings up (project name TBD). Baseline = no skill. Assertions check compose/container discovery, backend detection, correct pprof access (exec vs host port), and safety (read-only).

evals.name: docker-find-the-bottleneck
evals.prompt: I've got Teranode running locally in docker-compose and throughput seems low. Figure out which part of the pipeline is the bottleneck.
evals.expected_output: Discovers the compose project + teranode containers, pulls goroutine profiles (via host port where published, else docker exec), reads docker stats, names a bottleneck layer with evidence, frames directionally without an absolute TPS target.
assertions: Discovers the compose project and teranode containers (does not assume fixed names)
Pulls goroutine profiles (via docker exec or a published host port)
Names a specific bottleneck layer with evidence
Does NOT assert an absolute 'good/expected' TPS number
Performs no mutating docker/compose operations (no restart/down/up/rm)

evals.name: docker-health-backend-fsm
evals.prompt: Check my local dockerized Teranode stack — is it healthy? Which datastore is it using, and what's the FSM state?
evals.expected_output: Detects the UTXO backend correctly (Aerospike vs Postgres, e.g. from settings context / container presence), reports the FSM state, and checks the relevant datastore + Kafka health.
assertions: Correctly identifies the UTXO store backend in use
Checks the relevant datastore health (the actual backend, not blindly Aerospike)

evals.name: docker-stuck-service
evals.prompt: My local Teranode in docker seems stuck — a service isn't doing anything and there are no obvious errors. Help me find why.
evals.expected_output: Checks FSM state and Kafka/Redpanda consumer lag (Teranode pauses on unhealthy Kafka), scans container logs for errors, and points at the stalled service.
assertions: Checks Kafka/Redpanda health or consumer lag
Identifies the stalled service / a concrete next step
